name: Conservation of Plants and Animals
subject: biology
summary: Deforestation, wildlife sanctuaries, biosphere reserves, endemic and endangered species — why biodiversity matters.

crushed.title: Deforestation = cutting forests
crushed.text: Causes: farming, urbanisation, mining, dams. Effects: floods, drought, climate change, species loss.

crushed.title: Protected areas
crushed.text: Wildlife sanctuary (protect animals), National park (protect entire ecosystem), Biosphere reserve (research + conservation).

crushed.title: Endemic = found only here
crushed.text: Species unique to one geographic area. E.g. sal tree in MP, wild mango in Himalayas.

crushed.title: Endangered = near extinction
crushed.text: Very few left — tiger, one-horned rhino, Asiatic lion. Red Data Book lists them.

crushed.title: Migration
crushed.text: Birds fly long distances seasonally — Siberian crane to Bharatpur wetlands.

items.label: Wildlife sanctuary
items.detail: Animals protected from hunting. Human activity limited

items.label: National park
items.detail: Entire ecosystem protected. No grazing, logging, or private ownership

items.label: Biosphere reserve
items.detail: Large area for conservation + research + sustainable development

items.label: Zoo
items.detail: Ex-situ conservation — animals kept outside natural habitat

flows.title: Chain Reaction of Deforestation
flows.description: Cutting forests triggers a cascade of environmental problems.

steps.label: Trees cut
steps.description: Forests cleared for agriculture, roads, or industry.

steps.label: Soil exposed
steps.description: Without tree roots, topsoil washes away in rain.

steps.label: Less CO₂ absorbed
steps.description: Fewer trees means more CO₂ stays in atmosphere.

steps.label: Habitat lost
steps.description: Animals lose homes — some go extinct locally.

steps.label: Water cycle disrupted
steps.description: Less transpiration → less rain. Bare land → faster runoff → floods.

keyTerms.definition: Variety of plant and animal life in a region
Species found only in a particular area
keyTerms.analogy: A local specialty you can't find anywhere else

keyTerms.definition: Community of living organisms interacting with their environment
keyTerms.analogy: A neighbourhood where everyone depends on each other
An emergency contact list for species about to disappear

demos.title: Deforestation Chain Reaction
demos.description: Cutting forests triggers soil erosion and rising CO₂ in the atmosphere.

steps.label: Healthy forest
steps.description: Tree roots hold soil; leaves absorb CO₂ during photosynthesis.

steps.label: Trees cleared
steps.description: Logging removes cover — rain washes topsoil away.

steps.label: Erosion & CO₂ rise
steps.description: Bare land erodes; less CO₂ absorbed → greenhouse effect worsens.

chapterSummary.overview: Earth's biodiversity is under threat from deforestation, poaching, and pollution. This chapter explains why we must conserve forests and wildlife, how national parks and biosphere reserves protect species, and what individuals can do to reduce their ecological footprint.
keyPoints: Deforestation causes soil erosion, floods, loss of habitat, and increased CO₂ (global warming).
Wildlife conservation prevents extinction and maintains food chains and ecological balance.
Protected areas: wildlife sanctuaries, national parks, and biosphere reserves.
Endemic, endangered, and extinct species — Red Data Book lists threatened organisms.
Reforestation replants trees; we can reduce paper use, avoid products from endangered animals.
chapterSummary.examFocus: Explain consequences of deforestation. Differentiate sanctuary vs national park. List steps for conservation.
